title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite popular discourse, citing Cursor's semantic search improvements and rising Google search volume. He redefines RAG as iterative combination of vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used by agents, and frames the choice to cache embeddings as a tradeoff based on query volume.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer counters the claim that RAG is dead, pointing to Cursor's semantic search improvements and rising Google search volume as evidence. The talk redefines RAG as an iterative combination of vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters that agents call as tools, rather than a single retrieval step. It frames caching embeddings as a tradeoff governed by query volume: higher volume favors precomputed embeddings, lower volume favors computing them at query time.
